steps.title: Interpret the equation
steps.content: The equation is $$6x+21y=3{,}918.$$ The total tree count (3,918) equals (6 hectares)$\cdot x$ + (21 hectares)$\cdot y$. For units to work out as trees, $x$ and $y$ must each have units of trees per hectare. The coefficient $6$ comes from the community garden, so $x$ is the average trees per hectare in the community garden.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A: Average trees per hectare in the community garden — matches the analysis (6 hectares $\cdot$ trees/hectare = total community-garden trees).
  • B: Average trees per hectare in the public park — that role is played by $y$ (paired with 21 hectares), not $x$.
  • C: Total trees in the community garden — that would be $6x$, not $x$ itself.
  • D: Total trees in the public park — that would be $21y$, not $x$.
A is correct.
